summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 9 warning, 9 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: ЁЯЯб `bin/lib/extensions.mjs:177-183` тАФ Circuit-breaker logic is duplicated between `callHook` and `runExecuteRun`; if threshold or behavior changes, both must be updated; consider extracting a `trackFailure(ext)` helper
ЁЯЯб `bin/lib/run.mjs:371` тАФ `if (registry)` guard is a dead branch; `loadExtensions()` always returns `{ extensions: [] }` at minimum

ЁЯЯб `bin/lib/extensions.mjs`:169-187 тАФ Circuit-breaker logic in `runExecuteRun` is a manual copy of `callHook`'s L88-108 pattern. If the threshold or behavior changes in one place, the other could diverge. Consider extracting a shared helper or adding a cross-reference comment.
ЁЯЯб `.team/features/extension-system/tasks/task-6/artifacts/` тАФ Empty artifacts directory; no test-output.txt captured as evidence of verification

ЁЯЯб `test/extension-system.test.mjs` тАФ No test exercises the circuit breaker lifecycle through the `runExecuteRun` code path specifically; only tested via `callHook`; since `runExecuteRun` duplicates the circuit-breaker logic, it could regress independently

ЁЯЯб `bin/lib/extensions.mjs:177` тАФ `runExecuteRun` has a duplicated circuit-breaker code path with no test exercising the full 3-failure тЖТ disabled lifecycle. Add a test that calls `runExecuteRun` 4 times with a throwing hook and asserts the 4th call skips invocation.

ЁЯЯб `test/extension-system.test.mjs:205` тАФ No cross-hook circuit-breaker test. 3 failures in `promptAppend` should disable the extension for `executeRun`/`artifactEmit` too, but no test proves it. Regression risk if someone refactors to per-hook tracking.

ЁЯЯб `test/extension-system.test.mjs:205` тАФ Circuit-breaker disablement warning message (`"disabled after 3 consecutive failures"`) is not asserted in any test. Add `console.warn` capture to the circuit-breaker test.

ЁЯЯб `bin/lib/extensions.mjs:257` тАФ Size check uses `String.length` (characters) against `MAX_ARTIFACT_BYTES` (bytes); multi-byte UTF-8 content could exceed the 10MB limit by up to 4x; use `Buffer.byteLength()` instead
No critical (ЁЯФ┤) or warning (ЁЯЯб) findings. Zero veto-category violations found:
